title: Japanese-Style Braised Pork Shoulder with Tunisian Spices
description: A fusion of Japanese and North African flavors, this slow-cooked pork shoulder is tender and flavorful. The Tunisian spices add a warm, aromatic flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: tunisian spice blend
ingredients.notes: available at most Middle Eastern markets

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: used to add a savory flavor

ingredients.name: rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: used to add a tangy flavor

instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Season the pork shoulder with the Tunisian spice blend, salt, and brown sugar.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and water to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Braise the pork for 2 hours, or until tender and easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the pork with two forks, and toss it with the crumbly topping.
Serve the pork hot, garnished with green onions.
Optional: serve with steamed rice or noodles.
